# Test fixture provenance

- `tinyllama_tokenizer.json` / `tinyllama_tokenizer_config.json` — from
  [TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0](https://huggingface.co/TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0)
  (`tokenizer.json` / `tokenizer_config.json`, commit `fe8a4ea`). Apache License 2.0. TinyLlama
  is the model chosen in `TODO.md`'s Open Questions as the first end-to-end target — its tokenizer
  ("exactly the same architecture and tokenizer as Llama 2" per the model card) is what
  `tests/test_tokenizer.cpp` validates `Tokenizer` against, including byte-fallback BPE,
  CJK/emoji/accented text, and the model's chat-prompt format. Every expected token id list /
  decoded string in that test file was independently confirmed against the real `tokenizers`
  Python package (`Tokenizer.from_file(...).encode()`/`.decode()`) — and the chat-prompt shape
  against the real `chat_template` rendered with `jinja2` using HuggingFace's default `Environment`
  settings — before being hardcoded, not assumed.

- `tinyllama_config.json` — from
  [TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0](https://huggingface.co/TinyLlama/TinyLlama-1.1B-Chat-v1.0)
  (`config.json`). Apache License 2.0. Validates `loadLlamaConfigFromFile()`
  (`test_llama_config_reader.cpp`) against the real hyperparameters of the actual target model
  (32 attention heads, 4 KV heads — GQA — 22 layers, etc.), not a synthetic stand-in.

- `test_tensors.safetensors` / `test_tensors.gguf`, `llama_test_weights.safetensors` /
  `llama_test_weights_bf16.safetensors`, `gpt_test_weights.safetensors` — synthetic, generated by
  the real `safetensors`/`gguf` Python packages (`generate_*.py` scripts in this directory; the
  `_bf16` variant's generator hand-writes the safetensors binary format directly, since neither
  numpy nor the installed `safetensors` Python binding can write BF16 tensors).
